questions.question: The Department of the Air Force (DAF) operates under the authority, direction, and control of which official?
options: The Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
The President of the United States
The Secretary of the Air Force

questions.question: Who is the head of the Department of the Air Force and responsible for all affairs of the department?
options: The Secretary of the Air Force
The Chief of Staff of the Air Force
The Under Secretary of the Air Force
The Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff

questions.question: The Secretary of the Air Force (SecAF) is a civilian appointed by the President and confirmed by which body?
What is the role of the Chief of Staff of the Air Force (CSAF)?
options: Commands all deployed Air Force combat units
Serves as the senior uniformed Air Force officer and a member of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
Serves as the civilian head of the Air Force
Oversees only Air Force acquisition programs

questions.question: The Chief of Space Operations (CSO) serves as the senior uniformed officer of which organization?
Which official serves as the principal deputy and senior advisor to the Secretary of the Air Force?
options: The Chief of Staff of the Air Force
The Vice Chief of Staff of the Air Force
The Chief Master Sergeant of the Air Force
The Under Secretary of the Air Force

questions.question: Assistant Secretaries of the Air Force are responsible for which of the following functional areas?
options: Acquisition, Technology, and Logistics; Financial Management and Comptroller; Manpower and Reserve Affairs; and Installations, Environment, and Energy
Intelligence, Operations, Plans, and Personnel only
Maintenance, Supply Chain, and Civil Engineering only
Public Affairs, Legislative Liaison, and Protocol only

questions.question: The Air Staff is headquartered at which location?
What is the primary purpose of the Air Staff?
options: To command operational Air Force units in combat
To manage all Air Force installations worldwide
To furnish professional assistance to the Secretary of the Air Force and the Chief of Staff
To serve as the Air Force's primary training organization

questions.question: Which Major Command (MAJCOM) is responsible for organizing, training, and equipping combat airpower for air superiority, global attack, and combat search and rescue?
Air Mobility Command (AMC) is primarily responsible for which Air Force mission?
options: Rapid global mobility, including airlift and air refueling

questions.question: Which MAJCOM is responsible for recruiting, training, and educating Air Force personnel?
Air Force Materiel Command (AFMC) is headquartered at which installation?
Which MAJCOM is responsible for the Air Force's nuclear-capable bomber and intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) forces?
Air Force Special Operations Command (AFSOC) provides what type of specialized airpower?
options: Special operations capabilities including close air support, precision strike, and infiltration/exfiltration
Research, development, and acquisition of weapons systems

questions.question: Pacific Air Forces (PACAF) is the Air Force component command for which geographic combatant command?
United States Air Forces in Europe - Air Forces Africa (USAFE-AFAFRICA) provides airpower to which two combatant commands?
options: U.S. European Command and U.S. Africa Command
U.S. Central Command and U.S. Southern Command
U.S. Transportation Command and U.S. Strategic Command
U.S. Indo-Pacific Command and U.S. Northern Command

questions.question: Where is Air Education and Training Command (AETC) headquartered?
A Numbered Air Force (NAF) is an echelon of command directly below which organizational level?
What is the primary role of a Numbered Air Force (NAF)?
options: Serving as the Air Force's primary recruiting organization
Providing operational leadership and supervision over assigned units
Managing all Air Force financial accounts

questions.question: The wing is the basic unit of the Air Force that is capable of what?
options: Operating independently with only fighter aircraft
Providing only administrative and logistical functions
Self-sustaining operations in a deployed or home-station environment
Commanding all forces within a geographic area of responsibility

questions.question: Which type of wing contains diverse mission aircraft organized under a single wing commander?
An objective wing is structured to accomplish which of the following?
options: Multiple diverse flying missions under one commander
A single mission with one type of weapon system organized into an operations group, maintenance group, mission support group, and medical group
Only base operating support with no flying mission
Joint operations between the Air Force and Army

questions.question: An air base wing provides support and base services at a location where no flying wing is present. What does it typically NOT have?
options: An operations group with assigned aircraft

questions.question: The four standard groups within an objective wing are operations, maintenance, mission support, and which other?
The operations group within a wing is responsible for which primary function?
options: Providing food service and lodging for all wing personnel
Maintaining all wing facilities and infrastructure
Employing the weapon systems and conducting the wing's flying or operational mission
Managing the wing's financial and contracting activities

questions.question: Which group within a wing is responsible for generating and sustaining aircraft to meet mission requirements?
The mission support group within a wing provides which of the following functions?
options: Base operating support including security forces, civil engineering, communications, force support, and contracting

questions.question: What is the basic unit of the Air Force?
Flights are the smallest organizational units in the Air Force and are subdivisions of which echelon?
Direct Reporting Units (DRUs) are organizationally subordinate to which level of command?
options: The Headquarters Air Force (HAF) or the Chief of Staff of the Air Force

questions.question: What distinguishes a Direct Reporting Unit (DRU) from a Field Operating Agency (FOA)?
options: DRUs are always larger than FOAs
FOAs command combat forces while DRUs do not
DRUs perform specialized missions with mission authority not found in any existing MAJCOM, while FOAs perform field activities beyond the scope of any MAJCOM
There is no difference; the terms are interchangeable

questions.question: Field Operating Agencies (FOAs) report to which level of the Air Force?
options: A functional manager at Headquarters Air Force (HAF)

questions.question: Air Force Reserve Command (AFRC) is which type of organization?
The Air National Guard (ANG) has a unique dual mission under which two authorities?
options: Federal (Title 10 U.S. Code) and State (Title 32 U.S. Code) authority
Department of Defense and Department of Homeland Security authority

questions.question: When the Air National Guard is called to federal service (federalized), its members serve under which title of the U.S. Code?
Under state authority, ANG units can be activated by which official to respond to natural disasters or civil emergencies?
options: The Secretary of the Air Force

questions.question: Where is Air Combat Command (ACC) headquartered?
Air Force Global Strike Command (AFGSC) is headquartered at which installation?
options: Minot Air Force Base, North Dakota
F.E. Warren Air Force Base, Wyoming

questions.question: The Department of the Air Force includes which two military services?
options: The Air Force and the Army Air Corps
The Air Force and the Marine Corps
The Air Force and the Air National Guard
The United States Air Force and the United States Space Force
